intro:
Unfortunately, I could not find any information about the Tragacantha deluensis plant. However, I found that Tragacanth (also known as Tragacanth gum or Tragacantha deluensis is actually another name for Astragalus gummifer  or  Tragacanth is actually the dried sap of the plant  Astragalus gummifer. Here is a paragraph about Astragalus gummifer, the plant that produces Tragacanth:

Astragalus gummifer, also known as Tragacanth, is a perennial plant native to the Middle East and Eastern Europe, whose dried sap has been used as a natural adhesive and as a pharmaceutical excipient. The health benefits of Tragacanth include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and act as a demulcent, providing a protective barrier to mucous membranes. The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include mucilages, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Tragacanth include syrups, suspensions, and powders, often used in traditional medicine to treat coughs, sore throats, and gastrointestinal disorders.
